task_completion.description: Agent opens the Dockerfile for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) is visible showing the contents of the Dockerfile in the /home/ga/secure-build/ directory.

task_completion.description: Dockerfile is updated to use BuildKit secret mounting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Dockerfile contains a RUN instruction that includes the '--mount=type=secret' flag (e.g., RUN --mount=type=secret,id=mytoken).

task_completion.description: Dockerfile contains the logic to download the proprietary library using the secret token.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Dockerfile shows a curl or wget command that reads the mounted secret (e.g., $(cat /run/secrets/mytoken)) to set the 'X-Auth-Token' header, downloading from 'http://host.docker.internal:8090/proprietary_lib.tar.gz' and saving it to '/app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz'.

task_completion.description: Agent runs docker build with the secret flag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the execution of a 'docker build' command that includes the '--secret' flag referencing the auth_token.txt file (e.g., --secret id=mytoken,src=auth_token.txt).

task_completion.description: Agent tags the docker image correctly during the build.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'docker build' command in the terminal includes the argument '-t secure-app:latest'.
The terminal output shows the final stages of the Docker build process completing without errors, typically ending with 'DONE' or 'exported to image' for 'secure-app:latest'.

integrity.description: The agent must not hardcode the plaintext token into the Dockerfile.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Dockerfile should read the token dynamically from the mounted secret path (e.g., /run/secrets/...) and must not contain a hardcoded alphanumeric token string.

integrity.description: The agent must actually download the artifact, not fake its creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Dockerfile must not contain commands like 'touch /app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz' or 'echo ... > /app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz' intended to bypass the actual network download.

integrity.description: The Docker build must be genuinely executed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should display standard Docker BuildKit output (e.g., blue/gray progress bars, layer caching steps, #1, #2, etc.) rather than a simple echoed text or script output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/secure-build. The token is located in auth_token.txt. The server is hosted at http://host.docker.internal:8090/proprietary_lib.tar.gz. The final image must be tagged secure-app:latest and contain the artifact exactly at /app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz. The Dockerfile MUST use BuildKit secrets (RUN --mount=type=secret...) to pass the token to the download command to prevent it from leaking into the image history.
